originalText: A recent meta-analysis claims that a daily intake of 2000 IU of vitamin D reduces the risk of type 2 diabetes by 30%. It also states that more than 70% of adults are currently deficient in vitamin D and that supplementation completely eliminates seasonal flu.

claims.claimText: 2000 IU of vitamin D reduces risk of type 2 diabetes by 30%
more than 70% of adults are currently deficient in vitamin D

sources.name: New England Journal of Medicine
sources.evidence: Large RCT shows 26–32% relative risk reduction in high-risk groups.

sources.name: Global Nutrition Report
sources.evidence: Vitamin D deficiency prevalence varies widely by region and age.

sources.name: CDC Seasonal Flu Guidelines
sources.evidence: Supplementation may support immunity but does not eliminate flu risk.

verificationResults.summary: Overstates the benefits of supplementation beyond available evidence.
